name: Rhaenys Targaryen
disambiguator: Aegon I's younger sister-wife; mother of Aenys; slain in Dorne with her dragon Meraxes

name: Visenya Targaryen
disambiguator: Aegon I's elder sister-wife; mother of Maegor; later the Dowager Queen

name: Aenys Targaryen (Aenys I)
disambiguator: eldest son of Aegon I and Rhaenys; b. 7 AC; heir apparent; crowned Aenys I in 37 AC

name: Maegor Targaryen
disambiguator: son of Aegon I and Visenya; b. 12 AC; "Prince of Dragonstone"; Aenys's halfbrother

name: Rhaena Targaryen
disambiguator: eldest daughter of Aenys and Alyssa; b. 23 AC; named for Rhaenys; dragonrider of Dreamfyre

name: Aegon Targaryen (son of Aenys)
disambiguator: eldest son of Aenys and Alyssa; b. 26 AC; named after his grandsire Aegon I

name: Viserys Targaryen (son of Aenys)
disambiguator: second son of Aenys and Alyssa; b. 29 AC

name: Jaehaerys Targaryen (son of Aenys)
disambiguator: Aenys and Alyssa's fourth child, third son; b. 34 AC

name: Alysanne Targaryen (dau. of Aenys)
disambiguator: daughter of Aenys and Alyssa; b. 36 AC

name: Vaella Targaryen
disambiguator: infant daughter of Aenys and Alyssa; b. 39 AC; died in the cradle

name: Manfred Hightower
disambiguator: Lord of Oldtown; father of Ceryse ("not to be confused with his grandsire of the same name")

name: High Septon (Aenys's reign)
disambiguator: office of Oldtown; uncle of Ceryse; anointed Aenys; denounced Maegor's second marriage

name: High Septon (crowned Aegon)
disambiguator: the High Septon who crowned Aegon the Conqueror; remained until his death in 11 AC

name: Grand Maester Gawen
disambiguator: Grand Maester; chronicler of the court; proclaimed Aenys king

name: Ronnel Arryn
disambiguator: Lord of the Vale; deposed and imprisoned by his brother Jonos; flew thrice about the Giant's Lance

name: Vulture King
disambiguator: Dornish pretender of the Red Mountains; besieged the Marches; died of thirst and exposure

name: Samwell Tarly
disambiguator: Lord of Horn Hill; "Savage Sam"; wielder of the Valyrian blade Heartsbane

name: Septon Murmison
disambiguator: pious cleric said to heal by laying on of hands; Aenys's Hand after Maegor

name: Balerion
disambiguator: the Black Dread, greatest of the dragons; Aegon I's then Maegor's mount

name: Blackfyre
disambiguator: ancestral Valyrian steel sword; passed Aegon→Aenys→Maegor

name: Dark Sister
disambiguator: Visenya's Valyrian steel blade; given to Maegor on his 13th nameday

name: Heartsbane
disambiguator: Samwell Tarly's great Valyrian steel blade

name: Dragonstone
disambiguator: Targaryen seat; hatchlings born in its pits

name: King's Landing
disambiguator: grown to a city of a hundred thousand; site of the Iron Throne and Aegonfort

name: Aegonfort
disambiguator: Aegon's timber castle on the High Hill; torn down for the Red Keep

name: Red Keep
disambiguator: new stone castle ordered by Aegon on the Aegonfort's site

name: Harrenhal
disambiguator: riverlands castle granted to the Qoherys line; seized by Harren the Red

name: Oldtown
disambiguator: seat of the Hightowers; home of the Starry Sept; larger than King's Landing

name: Starry Sept
disambiguator: Oldtown sept where Maegor wed Ceryse and Aenys was anointed

name: Riverrun
disambiguator: Tully seat; site of the 28 AC tourney

name: Highgarden
disambiguator: where Aenys was on progress when Aegon died

name: Storm's End
disambiguator: Baratheon seat on the progress route

name: the Eyrie
disambiguator: Arryn seat; impregnable; site of the Moon Door

name: Moon Door
disambiguator: the Eyrie's fatal door through which Ronnel and Jonos were cast

name: Stonehelm
disambiguator: seat of House Swann; besieged by the Dornish

name: Nightsong
disambiguator: Caron seat the Vulture King failed to capture

name: Horn Hill
disambiguator: Tarly seat

name: Blackhaven
disambiguator: Dondarrion seat; stormed and burned by the Vulture King

name: Gods Eye
disambiguator: Harren the Red brought to bay in a village west of it

name: Sunspear
disambiguator: Deria Martell's seat; site of the "feast of friendship"

name: Pentos
disambiguator: free city Maegor departed to in exile

name: Stepstones
disambiguator: where Maegor fought Sargoso Saan

name: Driftmark
disambiguator: Velaryon seat; Larissa recalled there

name: Chamber of the Painted Table
disambiguator: Dragonstone chamber where Aegon died showing his grandsons his conquests

name: Kingsguard
disambiguator: knights who instructed Aenys and were unhorsed by Maegor

name: Faith of the Seven
disambiguator: the Faith; condemned Targaryen incest; led by the High Septon

name: House Targaryen
disambiguator: the royal house; Valyrian marriage custom of kin-to-kin

name: House Velaryon
disambiguator: ancient family of Valyrian stock; Aegon I's mother was a Velaryon

name: Evenstar of Tarth
disambiguator: title; Larissa Velaryon wed to his second son

name: village west of the Gods Eye
Harren the Red's seizure of Harrenhal
Maegor claims Balerion and takes the Eyrie
Wedding of Maegor and Alys Harroway
